Drawing title: INNER COMPONENT - BALL STUD.
Material: SAE J403 GRADE 1045 CD; minimum yield appears to be 690 MPa.
Metric dimensions and tolerances govern; English dimensions, if included, are for information only.
General note: ZINC NICKEL PLATE PER PS-152.17A.
General note: BREAK ALL SHARP EDGES 0.5 MAX.

coordinate_system.primary_axis: X axis is the central axis of revolution shown horizontally in SECTION A-A; +X is from the left annular end face toward the right end of the cylindrical shank.
coordinate_system.orientation_notes: The left circular view is an axial end view showing concentric circular features. SECTION A-A is a longitudinal section through the central axis. Because this is an axisymmetric component, feature access is recorded as axial or radial rather than plate-face +Z/-Z access.
coordinate_system.datum_origin: No formal datum origin is shown. The left end plane/left extension line is used as the informal dimensional reference for the 122.50 overall length and the 33.50 ball-location dimension.

features.name: Axisymmetric ball-stud body envelope
features.description: Overall rotational steel body consisting of a Ø40 cylindrical stem with a larger Ø63 spherical/bulged ball region and an axial through bore/counterbore system.

geometry.position_description: Overall part length along X is 122.50 mm; maximum radial envelope is Ø63.00 at the ball/bulged region.

relationships.detail: Small through bore and right-side counterbore share the central X axis.
Right-side counterbore is coaxial with the small through bore.
Central bore and Ø40 external cylinder share the same axis of revolution.
Ø40 external cylinder is coaxial with the central bore.
Counterbore and external shank are coaxial, producing a uniform annular wall at the right end.
External shank and counterbore share the same X axis.
Spherical/bulged ball surface is generated about the same X axis as the Ø40 cylinder.
Ø40 cylindrical stem is coaxial with the ball/bulged surface.
Left and right end faces are opposite axial end planes of the part.
Right and left end faces are opposite axial end planes of the part.
Small bore axis is normal to and opens through the left annular end face.
Right counterbore axis is normal to the right annular end face.
Internal annular shoulder is the bottom face formed by the right counterbore.
Internal counterbore shoulder face is parallel to the right end face.
Right end face is parallel to the internal counterbore shoulder face.
R2.0 blend radii modify the edges of the spherical/bulged ball surface.
R2.0 blend radii modify the transition between ball and Ø40 cylindrical stem.
Bond-prep zone lies on the outside ball/bulged surface region indicated by the leader.
Bond-prep zone extends/points near the ball-to-shank transition on the Ø40 stem side.
General edge-break requirement applies to the sharp edges of the finished body.
Central bore passes through the part body.
Right counterbore removes material from the body and intersects the right end.
External Ø40 cylinder is part of the axisymmetric body about X.
Part body envelope is coaxial with the Ø40 cylinder.
Spherical/bulged ball region is integral with the main body.
Left annular end face bounds the part body.
Right annular end face bounds the part body.
Internal shoulder face is normal to the central bore axis and surrounds the Ø19 bore opening into the counterbore.
[auto-enriched] Both F002 and F005 (flat_face) are accessed from the axial face

feature_graph.graph_summary: The drawing defines an axisymmetric steel inner ball-stud component, 122.50 mm long along its central X axis, with a maximum Ø63.00 spherical/bulged ball region located near the left side and a Ø40.00 external cylindrical stem/journal. The central passage consists of a Ø19.00 small through bore with limits Ø19.15/18.85, coaxial with a right-side Ø31.67 counterbore limited Ø31.77/31.57 and 50.00 mm nominal deep from the right end with limits 50.13/49.87. The ball/bulge has R31.50 geometry and is located 33.50 mm from the left reference plane, blending into the Ø40 stem with R2.0 circumferential radii. The part is SAE J403 Grade 1045 cold-drawn steel, zinc-nickel plated per PS-152.17A, with all sharp edges broken 0.5 mm maximum and a local bond-prep area called out on the external ball/stem region.

clarifications.question: Confirm whether the RFQ is for BS00B364350 only or also includes the BS00B364351 configuration/process row.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The parts table lists BS00B364350 as 'AS SHOWN' and BS00B364351 as 'PS-135.22 *'. The starred bond-prep process may apply only to the 351 variant, which affects required surface processing.
clarifications.suggested_default: Treat the RFQ item BS00B364350 as the as-shown geometry with zinc-nickel plating, and treat PS-135.22/bond-prep as conditional unless purchasing specifies BS00B364351.

clarifications.question: What are the exact axial and circumferential boundaries of the 'BOND PREP THIS AREA *' zone?
clarifications.why_it_matters: The leader identifies a local surface-treatment area but no numeric boundary dimensions are visible; exact coverage may affect inspection and masking/preparation requirements.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use the leader-indicated lower external spherical/transition surface only; do not assume full 360° coverage unless the process standard PS-135.22 defines it.

clarifications.question: Confirm the title-block general tolerance values for untoleranced metric dimensions.
clarifications.why_it_matters: The default tolerance block is small and partially legible. Explicit limit dimensions are clear, but untoleranced dimensions such as Ø63.00, R31.50, R2.0, 33.50, and 122.50 depend on the default tolerance standard.
clarifications.suggested_default: Use explicit limit dimensions where present; for untoleranced dimensions use the title-block default as best-read: approximately X.X ±0.5, X.XX ±0.25, angles ±2°.

guardrail_results.message: Title block, parts/process table, material, coating note, and general edge-break note were reviewed before feature extraction.
No ordinate dimensioning was present. Overall envelope was derived from the explicit overall length 122.50 and maximum diameter Ø63.00.
Left axial end view and longitudinal SECTION A-A were identified. The A-A letters are section-cut labels, not datum symbols.
Full drawing scan found no metric or other thread callouts such as M2, M3, M4, M5, M6, M8, M10, or M12.
All visible diameter callouts with Ø symbols were reviewed. The drawing contains coaxial bores/counterbore and external diameters, not hole patterns or repeated fastener holes.
Counterbore depth was extracted from 50.13/49.87. Small bore is through by section continuity and opens through the counterbored passage. No slots or pockets are present.
No formal datum feature symbols or GD&T frames were identified. A-A is treated only as a section view.
Every feature has at least one relationship, symmetric coaxial/opposite/parallel relationships are paired, and no duplicate feature IDs are present.
The process applicability for PS-135.22/bond-prep and the exact local bond-prep zone boundaries are not fully defined on the raster image.

analysis_trace.summary: Single landscape drawing page with parts/process table at upper left, main end view and SECTION A-A in the center, notes at lower left, and title block at lower right.
issues: Small title-block tolerance text is partly legible.
The drawing covers at least two suffix part numbers, BS00B364350 and BS00B364351.

analysis_trace.summary: Identified the left circular end view as an axial projection and SECTION A-A as the longitudinal section through the part centerline. The A-A labels are section-cut markers, not datum feature symbols.

Extracted the axisymmetric body, left and right end faces, Ø19.00 small through bore, Ø31.67 right counterbore, internal counterbore shoulder, Ø40.00 external cylindrical stem, Ø63/R31.50 spherical-bulged ball surface, R2.0 blend radii, local bond-prep zone, and general edge break.
issues: Left neck/transition exact tangent locations are not separately dimensioned beyond R31.50, R2.0, 33.50, and Ø40 dimensions.

analysis_trace.summary: Correlated the concentric circles in the left end view with the coaxial features shown in SECTION A-A: outer ball projection Ø63, Ø40 stem/end circle, and Ø19 central bore.

Constructed an axisymmetric feature graph with coaxial bore/counterbore/cylinder/ball relationships, opposite end faces, internal shoulder, transition fillets, local surface-treatment zone, and parent body relationships.

Embedded all clearly visible limit dimensions into the constrained features: Ø40.13/39.87, Ø19.15/18.85, Ø31.77/31.57, and 50.13/49.87. Untoleranced dimensions carry a general-title-block-tolerance note.
issues: Default tolerance values were only partially legible, so they were recorded at part level with a clarification.

analysis_trace.summary: Scanned for datum feature symbols and GD&T frames. None were found. The A-A symbol is used only for a section view.
Extracted SAE J403 Grade 1045 CD material, zinc-nickel plating per PS-152.17A, general sharp-edge break 0.5 max, metric-dimension note, and process table entries for BS00B364350/351.
issues: Applicability of PS-135.22/starred bond-prep to the RFQ part should be confirmed.

analysis_trace.summary: Completed thread check, hole/bore completeness check, depth check, dimension re-read for lower-confidence items, relationship completeness review, and envelope sanity check. No thread callouts or hole patterns are present; all Ø callouts were mapped to rotational features.
issues: Exact bond-prep zone boundary and title-block default tolerance details remain clarification items.
